EndoscopicImage.title: EndoscopicImage
EndoscopicImage.description: Represents an endoscopic image uploaded by a medical user for AI analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EndoscopicImage entity.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the user who uploaded the image. (Relationship: User 1:N EndoscopicImage)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded image file.

storagePath.description: The URL or path where the image is stored in the cloud storage (e.g., Firebase Storage).

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the uploaded image file (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'image/png').

uploadTimestamp.description: The timestamp when the image was uploaded.

thumbnailStoragePath.description: Optional URL or path to a generated thumbnail of the image, if available.

PredictionResult.title: PredictionResult
PredictionResult.description: Stores the AI diagnostic prediction results for a specific endoscopic image.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PredictionResult entity.

imageId.description: Reference to the EndoscopicImage that this prediction corresponds to. (Relationship: EndoscopicImage 1:N PredictionResult)

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the user who initiated this prediction. (Relationship: User 1:N PredictionResult)

predictionTimestamp.description: The timestamp when the AI prediction was generated.

overallPrediction.description: The final, aggregated disease prediction based on model voting (e.g., 'Polyp', 'Ulcer', 'Healthy').

overallConfidence.description: The confidence score (as a percentage or decimal) for the overall prediction.

status.description: A textual status indicating the prediction outcome (e.g., 'Detected', 'Not Detected', 'Review Required').

modelOutputsJson.description: A JSON string representing an array of objects, where each object contains an individual AI model's prediction and confidence score (e.g., [{'modelName': 'VGG16', 'prediction': 'Polyp', 'confidence': 0.72}]).

AIModel.title: AIModel
AIModel.description: Stores metadata and performance metrics for the deep learning models used in the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIModel entity. This is typically the model's name.

name.description: The official name of the AI model (e.g., 'VGG16', 'ResNet50', 'InceptionV3').

description.description: A brief description of the AI model, including its architecture or purpose.

accuracyBeforeTuning.description: The accuracy percentage of the model before hyperparameter tuning.

accuracyAfterTuning.description: The accuracy percentage of the model after hyperparameter tuning.

lastUpdated.description: The timestamp when the model's performance metrics were last updated.

definition.description: Stores endoscopic images uploaded by a specific user. The 'uploadedByUserId' field in the document must match the '{userId}' path parameter for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns and uploaded the image.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific endoscopic image.

definition.description: Stores AI prediction results associated with a specific endoscopic image, owned by a specific user. The 'uploadedByUserId' field in the document must match the '{userId}' path parameter for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the associated image and initiated the prediction.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent endoscopic image.

params.name: predictionResultId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific AI prediction result.

definition.description: Stores metadata and performance metrics for the deep learning models. This collection is globally readable by authenticated users and is not user-specific.

params.name: modelId
params.description: The unique identifier for the AI model (e.g., 'VGG16').

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Accurate Permissions (QAPs) by leveraging path-based ownership and denormalization. User-specific data, such as `EndoscopicImage` and `PredictionResult`, are nested under `/users/{userId}` paths. This design ensures that all documents within these collections share the same security posture – they are exclusively owned by the `userId` defined in the path.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **`/users/{userId}/endoscopicImages/{imageId}`**: Each `EndoscopicImage` document includes an `uploadedByUserId` field. This field MUST be identical to the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This denormalization allows security rules to authorize access to an image simply by verifying that `request.auth.uid == userId`, without needing to perform a `get()` operation on a parent user document or cross-reference other collections. This ensures atomic operations and simplifies rule debugging.
2.  **`/users/{userId}/endoscopicImages/{imageId}/predictionResults/{predictionResultId}`**: Similarly, each `PredictionResult` document contains an `uploadedByUserId` field which MUST match the `{userId}` wildcard in its hierarchical path. This critical denormalization allows rules to validate access directly against `request.auth.uid == userId`, eliminating any `get()` calls to the parent `endoscopicImage` or root `user` document for authorization. This is crucial for maintaining performance and rule simplicity.

**Query-Accurate Permissions (QAPs):**
1.  **User-owned Data (`/users/{userId}/...`)**: By nesting `EndoscopicImage` and `PredictionResult` under the user's ID, `list` operations become inherently secure and performant. A user can only query and retrieve documents from their specific `/users/{request.auth.uid}/endoscopicImages` or `/users/{request.auth.uid}/endoscopicImages/{imageId}/predictionResults` paths. The security rules can simply enforce `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;`, guaranteeing that no user can inadvertently or maliciously retrieve another user's private medical data. The denormalized `uploadedByUserId` within the documents further reinforces this for collection group queries, if needed.
2.  **Global Data (`/aiModels/{modelId}`)**: The `AIModel` collection is designed as a top-level collection (`/aiModels`) with a read-only security posture for authenticated users. This segregation (Strategy B) means that all documents in this collection have the same access requirements: authenticated users can read, but not write. `list` operations are therefore inherently secure, as they are intended to display global, public model information, and rules can simply state `allow read: if request.auth.uid != null;`.

This structure adheres to structural segregation by clearly separating private, user-specific data from global, read-only data. It uses consistent naming for wildcards and denormalized fields (e.g., `uploadedByUserId`) to enhance clarity, debuggability, and enforce atomic authorization without complex `get()` calls, directly addressing the core design principles.
